name: Secondary Organic Aerosol Formation
description: Secondary organic aerosol forms when volatile organic compounds are oxidized by OH, ozone, or nitrate radicals into lower-volatility products; once these vapors are supersaturated they partition onto existing particles or nucleate new ones, then grow by condensation and aging to build atmospheric aerosol mass.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["VOC emissions"]
    N2["Oxidation by OH/O3/NO3"]
    N3["Lower-volatility products"]
    N4{"Vapors supersaturated?"}
    N5["Partition to particles"]
    N6["New particle nucleation"]
    N7["Condensational growth"]
    N8["Aging / oligomerization"]
    N9["Secondary organic aerosol"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|no: oxidize more| N2
    N4 -->|yes| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

sources.title: The formation, properties and impact of secondary organic aerosol: current and emerging issues
Atmospheric Chemistry and Physics: From Air Pollution to Climate Change (3rd ed.)
